nature.meaning: A tall plant with a wooden trunk, branches, and leaves that lives for many years
nature.example: The big oak tree in our backyard gives us shade on sunny summer days.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Trees are tall plants that give us shade, fruit, and clean air to breathe.
nature.sentence: The tree's leaves turn orange and red in the fall.

nature.meaning: The colorful part of a plant that makes seeds and attracts bees and butterflies
nature.example: The yellow sunflower is as tall as me and has soft yellow petals.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Flowers are pretty, colorful plants. They smell nice and make seeds.
nature.sentence: The flower opened its petals to the morning sun.

nature.meaning: Green plants with narrow leaves that cover the ground in lawns and fields
nature.example: I run barefoot on the soft green grass and feel the cool blades under my feet.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Grass is the green carpet on the ground. It grows in yards, parks, and fields.
nature.sentence: The grass is wet with morning dew.

nature.meaning: The flat, green part of a plant or tree that uses sunlight to make food
nature.example: I jump into a big pile of crunchy leaves that fell from the maple tree.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Leaves are the green parts of trees and plants. They change color in the fall.
nature.sentence: The leaf floated down from the tree.

nature.meaning: A very high, steep piece of land that rises high above the surrounding area
nature.example: The mountain has snow on its top even in summer because it is so high and cold.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Mountains are very, very tall hills. Some have snow on top all year.
nature.sentence: We hiked to the top of the mountain.

nature.meaning: A raised area of land that is lower and less steep than a mountain
nature.example: We roll down the grassy hill and get dizzy but laugh the whole way.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: A hill is like a small mountain. It's fun to roll down hills!
nature.sentence: The hill was perfect for sledding in winter.

nature.meaning: A large natural flow of water that moves across land toward the ocean or another river
nature.example: The fish swim in the river and the water flows quickly over the rocks.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: A river is water that moves across land. It flows all the way to the ocean.
nature.sentence: The river was too deep to cross.

nature.meaning: A large body of water surrounded by land on all sides
nature.example: We swim in the lake, fish from the dock, and have picnics by the shore.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: A lake is like a big pond with water all around, surrounded by land.
nature.sentence: The lake was calm and still like a mirror.

nature.meaning: A very large body of salt water that covers most of the Earth's surface
nature.example: The ocean is so big you cannot see the other side, only water everywhere.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: The ocean is the big, salty water that covers most of our planet Earth.
nature.sentence: The waves crashed against the shore.

nature.meaning: A small body of still water, smaller than a lake
nature.example: The pond has lily pads, frogs, and tiny fish swimming near the edge.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: A pond is a small pool of water where frogs, ducks, and fish live.
nature.sentence: The ducks swam in the pond.

nature.meaning: A white or gray fluffy mass in the sky made of tiny water droplets
nature.example: The cloud looks like a bunny, and that one looks like an ice cream cone!
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Clouds are fluffy white things in the sky. They bring rain and snow.
nature.sentence: The dark clouds mean a storm is coming.

nature.meaning: Water that falls from clouds in drops to the ground
nature.example: I use my colorful umbrella and wear boots when the rain falls on my head.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Rain is water that falls from the sky. It helps plants grow.
nature.sentence: The rain tapped on the window.

nature.meaning: White frozen water that falls from clouds in soft flakes in cold weather
nature.example: We build a snowman and have a snowball fight when the snow covers the ground.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Snow is white, fluffy, cold stuff that falls in winter. You can make snowballs!
nature.sentence: The snow sparkled like tiny diamonds.

nature.meaning: Water that has frozen solid and become hard and cold
nature.example: I put ice cubes in my drink to make it cold on a hot summer day.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Ice is frozen water. It is hard and cold, like in an ice cube or on a frozen pond.
nature.sentence: The ice on the pond was slippery.

nature.meaning: The star at the center of our solar system that gives us light and heat
nature.example: The sun rises in the east every morning and sets in the west at night.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: The sun is the big, bright, yellow thing in the sky that makes daytime warm and light.
nature.sentence: Don't look directly at the sun; it hurts your eyes.

nature.meaning: The round object in the night sky that shines by reflecting the sun's light
nature.example: The moon looks like a banana when it is a crescent and a circle when it is full.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: The moon is the white or yellow circle in the night sky. It changes shape!
nature.sentence: The moon followed us home in the car.

nature.meaning: A huge ball of burning gas in space that looks like a tiny dot of light in the night sky
nature.example: I see a million stars twinkling in the dark sky on a clear night.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Stars are little lights in the night sky. They are actually giant balls of fire far away.
nature.sentence: Make a wish on the first star you see.

nature.meaning: The space above the earth that you see when you look up
nature.example: The sky is blue on a sunny day, gray when it rains, and orange at sunset.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: The sky is what you see when you look up. Clouds, the sun, and the moon are in the sky.
nature.sentence: The sky was painted with pink and orange at sunset.

nature.meaning: A hard piece of earth or stone
nature.example: I collect smooth, flat rocks from the beach and skip them across the water.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Rocks are hard pieces of earth. Some are big like boulders, some are small like pebbles.
nature.sentence: The rock was too heavy to lift.

nature.meaning: Tiny grains of rock and mineral found on beaches and in deserts
nature.example: I build a sandcastle with wet sand at the beach and decorate it with shells.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Sand is tiny, soft, grainy stuff on the beach. You can build castles with it when it's wet.
nature.sentence: The sand squished between my toes.

nature.meaning: Loose soil or earth, often used for planting
nature.example: I dig in the dirt with a shovel to plant flower seeds in our garden.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Dirt is soil on the ground where plants grow. Worms live in the dirt.
nature.sentence: My hands were covered in dirt after gardening.

nature.meaning: Wet, soft, sticky dirt or clay
nature.example: We make mud pies in the puddle after it rains and let them dry in the sun.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Mud is dirt mixed with water. It's wet, squishy, and fun to play in!
nature.sentence: Don't step in the mud with your new shoes.

nature.meaning: A small pool of water on the ground, usually after rain
nature.example: I jump in every puddle with my rain boots and watch the water splash.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: A puddle is a little pool of water on the ground after it rains. Jump in it!
nature.sentence: The puddle reflected the blue sky.

nature.meaning: A curved arc of colors that appears in the sky when sunlight shines through rain
nature.example: After the rain stopped, a beautiful rainbow appeared with red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: A rainbow has seven colors. It comes out after rain when the sun shines.
nature.sentence: There's a pot of gold at the end of the rainbow!

nature.meaning: Moving air that you can feel but not see
nature.example: The wind blows the leaves off the trees and makes my kite fly high.
nature.kid_friendly_explanation: Wind is moving air. You can feel it on your face but you can't see it.
nature.sentence: The wind howled through the trees.
